message:
fix(cancel): prevent reentrancy deadlock in SymbolCancelToken

The previous implementation held the listeners lock while iterating
and calling on_cancel callbacks. If a listener's callback attempted
to interact with the same cancel token (e.g., registering a new
listener or checking cancellation state), this would deadlock.

Solution: Extract listeners into a local Vec before iterating, releasing
the lock before any callbacks execute. Same pattern applied to child
token notification. This ensures:

- Callbacks can safely interact with the cancel token
- No lock held during potentially long-running notifications
- Listeners and children are still notified exactly once

The std::mem::take idiom efficiently moves the collection out of the
Mutex, leaving an empty Vec in place, with zero allocation overhead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
